Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 198 [Mis Pi 1-2 incantations][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

en₂ im kug-zu dasar-re abzu-a igi ma-an-⸢šum₂⸣

(o 1) Incantation: 'Asare (Akkadian has instead: Marduk) saw your pure clay in the Abzu.

ṭi-da-ka el-lu dAMAR.UTU ina ap-si-i ip-pa-lis-ma
o 2o 2

duraḫ-zu duraḫ za-gin₃-na ⸢ŋeštug₂⸣-zu ŋeštug₂ ab₂-šilam-x

(o 2) 'Your goat is a goat (Akkadian has instead: Your behaviour is the behaviour) of lapis lazuli. Your ear is the ear of a cow.

⸢tal-lak-ta⸣-ka tal-lak-tu₄ <uq>-⸢na⸣-tu₄ u₂-zu-un-ka u₂-zu-un lit-[ti]
o 3o 3

⸢duraḫ⸣-zu ⸢ŋiri₃⸣ ki-a sig₁₀-ga-zu im šutug ⸢šub⸣-ba

(o 3) 'For your goat (Akkadian has instead: your behaviour), your foot fixed to the ground, the clay for the reed-hut has been laid (Akkadian has instead: I laid the clay for the reed-hut).

ana ⸢tal-lak⸣-ti-ka še-pi-ka ša₂ ana KIti₃ kun-na ṭi-da ana ⸢šu-tuk ad-di⸣
o 44

eme ḫulu-ŋal₂ bar-⸢še₃ ḫe₂⸣-em-⸢ta⸣-gub-ba

(o 4) 'May the evil tongue stand aside.'

o 55

ka-inim-ma im šutug ⸢šub⸣-ba ka duḫ-⸢u₃⸣-[da-kam₂]

(o 5) It is the incantation-formula for laying the clay for the reed-hut (and) opening the mouth (of the god's statue).

o 6o 6

en₂ gi kug gi gid₂-da gi ŋeš-gi [x x]

(o 6) Incantation: 'Pure reed, long reed, reed from the [...] reed-thicket,

qa-nu-u el-lu qa-nu-u ar-ku qa-an ID₂ [...]
o 7o 7

gi bar-ra giu₂-šub gi⸢en₃?-bar? gi⸣-[šul]-⸢ḫi⸣

(o 7) 'reed peeled for the succulent part, enbar-reed, šulhi-reed,

qa-nu-u qal-pu ana a-da-⸢at-ti qa-an⸣ [x x] ⸢qa-an⸣ ša₂-⸢la⸣-[lu]
o 8o 8

⸢gi mu-a⸣-bi x [x x] x suḫ

(o 8) 'reed whose name is "... [...] ... tearing out",

[qa]-nu-u ša₂ šum-šu x [...] na-sa-ḫu
o 9o 9

gi dnamma gi nanše gi x [x x] x-ke₄

(o 9) 'reed of Namma, reed of Nanše, reed of ... [...] ...,

qa-an dNAMMU qa-an nanše qa-⸢an⸣ [...] x NIG₂
o 10o 10

gi dasar-lu₂-ḫi dumu eridug⸢ki⸣-ga-ke₄

(o 10) 'reed of Asarluhi (Akkadian has instead: Marduk), son of Eridug --

qa-an dAMAR.UTU mar ⸢eri-du₁₀⸣
o 1111

⸢šutug šub⸣-ba ⸢:⸣ ana? šu-⸢tuk⸣-ki ad-di

(o 11) 'laying (Akkadian has instead: I laid for) the reed-hut.

o 1212

eme ḫulu-ŋal₂ bar-še₃ ḫe₂-em-ta-gub-ba

(o 12) 'May the evil tongue stand aside.'

o 1313

en₂ gi abzu-ta de₆-a

(o 13) Incantation: 'Reed brought from the Abzu,

o 1414

gibuniŋ ban₃-da pad₃-da-am₃

(o 14) 'called "Junior bucket",

o 1515

dnamma mu še₂₁ den-ki nam tar-ra

(o 15) 'named by Namma (and) decreed a fate by Enki,

o 1616

gi gal gi urin-gal gi gurum-ma gi diŋir-re-e-ne-ke₄

(o 16) 'great reed, reed for the ritual hut, bent reed, reed of the gods,

o 1717

eš₃ abzu kug-ga-ta mi₂ zid-de₃-eš dug₄-ga

(o 17) 'righteously tended in the shrine of the pure Abzu --

o 1818

a id₂buranunki den-ki-ga-ke₄

(o 18) 'the Enki-gods (and) Ninki-goddesses took the Euphrates water of Enki in the reed (and then) took it from that reed.

o 1919

den-ki-e-ne dnin-ki-e-⸢ne⸣

o 2020

gi šu im-ma-an-teŋ₄ gi-bi-ta šu im-ma-an-teŋ₄

o 2121

⸢d⸣kug-sud saŋa₄-maḫ den-lil₂-la₂-ke₄ giurin-gal ḫe₂-x

(o 21) 'May Kugsud, the chief saŋa-priest of Enlil, ... the ritual hut.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[ka-inim]-⸢ma⸣ mu-un-du₃-du₃ nam-⸢šub⸣ [ba]-⸢an⸣-[šum₂]

(r 1) 'He has performed the incantation-formula. He has delivered the incantation.

r 22

[mu]-un-sikil-la mu-un-⸢dadag⸣-ga

(r 2) 'He has purified. He has cleaned.

r 33

eme ḫulu-ŋal₂ bar-še₃ ḫe₂-em-⸢ta⸣-gub

(r 3) 'May the evil tongue stand aside.'

r 44

ka-inim-ma giurin-gal-a-kam₂

(r 4) It is the incantation-formula for the ritual hut.

r 55

en₂ gi abzu-ta ⸢mu₂⸣-a

(r 5) Incantation: 'Reed grown from the Abzu,

r 66

gi eridugki-ga-ke₄ ⸢mi₂ zid⸣-de₃-eš ⸢dug₄⸣-ga

(r 6) 'righteously tended reed of Eridug,

r 77

gi ur₂-zu ki-še₃ a-ta? x x e₃-a

(r 7) 'reed, your roots have come forth to the earth ... from the water,

r 88

gi ki a gi kug-ga-ke₄ e₃-a

(r 8) 'reed come forth to a place of water among pure reeds,

r 99

gi šim-⸢me⸣-a-bi šeŋ₃ an-na-ke₄ ŋar-ra-<<ab>>

(r 9) 'reed whose fragrance the rain of heaven has laid down,

r 1010

gi pa-bi sud-sud gi dnamma gi dnanše

(r 10) 'reed whose stem is long, reed of Namma, reed of Nanše,

r 1111

gi til₃!(GAL₂-)la gi dnin-tin-ug₅-ga-ke₄

(r 11) 'reed of life, reed of Nintinuga --

r 1212

dasar-lu₂-ḫi dumu eridugki-ga-ke₄ na-de₅-ga-a-ni šu im-ma-an-teŋ₄

(r 12) 'Asarluhi, son of Eridug, took his incense.

r 1313

den-ki-ke₄ saŋ-ŋa₂ e₂ tu₅!(NAGA) a im-ma-an-de₆

(r 13) 'Enki, the head of the bath-house, brought water.

r 1414

gigilim giurin-gal ba-an-dab

(r 14) 'He held the reed bundles, the ritual hut.

r 1515

gi dasar-lu₂-ḫi šu im-ma-an-teŋ₃-ŋe₂₆

(r 15) 'Asarluhi was taking the reed.

r 1616

gi⸢urin⸣-gal nir-ra gi si sa₂-⸢ta⸣

(r 16) 'After the ritual hut had been set up (and) the reed arranged,

r 1717

[dkug]-⸢sud saŋa₄⸣-maḫ d⸢en-lil₂⸣-la₂-ke₄

(r 17) 'Kugsud, the chief saŋa-priest of Enlil --1

r 1818

[x] ⸢ḫulu-ŋal₂ bar⸣-še₃ ḫe₂-em-ta-gub

(r 18) 'May the evil [...] stand aside.'

r 1919

ka-inim-ma giurin-[gal] ⸢si⸣ sa₂-kam₂

(r 19) It is the incantation-formula for arranging the ritual hut.

r 2020

en₂ gi šag₄-ga sikil-la [x x] x x GA

(r 20) Incantation: 'Reed with a pure centre [...] ...,

r 2121

gi ŋeš-ḫur-ḫur-re diŋir-re-[e-ne]-ke₄

(r 21) 'reed for the magic circles of the gods,

r 2222

⸢gi⸣urin-gal x x NA-ke₄

(r 22) 'ritual hut ...,

r 2323

gi ŋeš-gi kug-ga U₂ x ⸢šar₂⸣-ra ⸢ensi-da?⸣ gub-ba

(r 23) 'reed of the pure reed-thicket, numerous ..., standing by the dream interpreter --

r 2424

dasar-lu₂-ḫi [x] ⸢im⸣-ma-an-šum₂

(r 24) 'Asarluhi gave [...] to it.

r 2525

dkug-sud saŋa₄-maḫ ⸢den-lil₂⸣-[la₂-ke₄ x] in-ši-in-⸢ŋar⸣

(r 25) 'Kugsud, the chief saŋa-priest of Enlil, placed [...] on it.

r 2626

gi kug ki ⸢kug⸣ gi ŋeš-gi KA x x rib-ba

(r 26) 'Pure reed, pure place, reed of the reed-thicket surpassing ...,

r 2727

ambar x sikil-la-⸢ta? mu₂-a⸣

(r 27) 'grown from a pure ... reed-bed --

r 2828

šu kug-ga AN MIN ŠU šu im-ma-an-teŋ₄ dnamma? si₁₂-⸢si₁₂-ga-am?-bi⸣

(r 28) 'Namma took the ... in pure hands, its greenery,

r 2929

gigilim x-a gub-ba ki-bi-še₃ giurin-gal ⸢ba⸣-[an]-⸢de₆⸣

(r 29) 'the reed bundles standing in ..., she brought the ritual hut to its place.

r 3030

⸢nam⸣-išib x [x] eridugki-ga mu-un-[x-x]

(r 30) 'She [...] the office of išib-priest ... [...] in Eridug.

r 3131

x ḫul ḫe₂-a x ḫul ḫe₂-a x ḫul ḫe₂-a gal₅-la₂ ḫul ḫe₂-a [x] A NU UN x x x

(r 31) 'Whether it be an evil ..., or an evil ..., or an evil ..., or an evil galla-demon, [...] ...,

r 3232

x AG A uš₁₁ ḫulu-ŋal₂ ḫe₂-a ki-bi-še₃ ib₂-ta-ed₂ ⸢nam⸣-erim₂ ib₂-ku₄-ku₄ tu₆

(r 32) '..., or evil sorcery, it shall leave for its place (and)) the oath enter.' Spell.

r 3333

ka-inim-ma gigilim du₃-du₃-a-bi

(r 33) Its incantation-formula for making reed bundles.

Top
(catchline)
t.e. 11

⸢en₂⸣ ŋeššinig ŋeš kug-ga ki sikil-ta mu₂-a

(t.e. 1) Incantation: 'Tamarisk, pure tree, grown from a pure place.'

(colophon)
t.e. 22

3-šu₂ nis-ḫu ša₂ an-na tu-ud-da-a ZA₃.TI.⸢LA⸣

(t.e. 2) 3rd excerpt from 'It (the divine statue) is born in heaven'. (Copied) to the end.

1 Presumably an abbreviation of obv . 21 - rev . 2.

Created by Graham Cunningham for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338517
